title: Exelixis — Relationships
html:
Exelixis — ZoomRx Relationship

Governing Thought

The ZoomRx-Exelixis relationship is a 7-year partnership that has evolved through distinct phases: a Cabozantinib PET origin (2019), a tracking and specialty research middle period (2023-2024), and a current reactivation driven by zanzalintinib pre-launch needs (2026). The relationship is not continuous — there are gaps — but the pattern is clear: Exelixis returns to ZoomRx when it needs market research support around its most important commercial moments. The current phase is the most strategically important because zanzalintinib is the company's future, and the research decisions being made now (ATU vendor, campaign testing partner, launch tracking vendor) will define multi-year vendor relationships through launch and beyond.

Partnership Timeline

Year Milestone Significance
2019 Cabozantinib PET launched (01 EXL_Cabo_PET_2019) ZoomRx's first engagement with Exelixis. Established the relationship through promotional effectiveness tracking on the anchor brand.
2023 Cabozantinib Integrated Tracking (02) Deepened the relationship from standalone PET to integrated tracking — suggesting Exelixis saw enough value to expand scope.
2023 AE Training (00) ZoomRx involved in adverse event reporting training — a non-standard engagement suggesting breadth beyond core MR.
2024 Exelixis Perxcept (03) Specialty research engagement. Perxcept is a ZoomRx proprietary methodology — Exelixis adopted a ZoomRx-specific tool, indicating trust in the firm's methodological differentiation.
2024-2025 Gap period No new projects visible on SharePoint. Likely corresponds to the period between cabozantinib maturity and zanzalintinib pipeline advancement.
Feb 2026 Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact (04) — Wave 01 + Wave 02 Reactivation. Exelixis returns to ZoomRx for zanzalintinib pre-launch campaign work. The unbranded campaign focus suggests disease awareness / market shaping activity ahead of approval.
Apr 2026 Zanzalintinib ATU RFP issued (v1.0) Exelixis is formally sourcing an ATU vendor for zanzalintinib. RFP sent to ZoomRx — active competitive bid.
Apr 2026 Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing proposal (v0.1, v0.2) ZoomRx has submitted proposals for branded campaign testing research. Two versions suggest iterative refinement based on client feedback — an engaged dialogue, not a cold pitch.

Key statistic: 6 project engagements across 7 years, with the current phase (2026) representing the highest strategic value due to zanzalintinib pre-launch timing.

Why Exelixis Works with ZoomRx

Pillar 1: Promotional Effectiveness Heritage

The relationship started with a Cabozantinib PET — ZoomRx's core bread-and-butter service. This means the foundational credibility is built on quantitative rigor: ZoomRx proved it could track promotional effectiveness in a competitive oncology market (RCC/HCC) and deliver actionable data on rep messaging, physician awareness, and prescribing behavior. Exelixis knows ZoomRx can do oncology PET work. That's not in question. The task now is to extend that credibility to adjacent services (ATU, campaign testing, launch tracking).

Evidence:

  • Cabozantinib PET (2019) was followed by Integrated Tracking (2023) — scope expansion signals satisfaction
  • Exelixis returned to ZoomRx after a gap, suggesting no negative experience drove the hiatus

Pillar 2: Methodological Trust (Perxcept Adoption)

Exelixis adopted Perxcept — a ZoomRx proprietary methodology — in 2024. This is significant because proprietary method adoption indicates trust in ZoomRx's intellectual property and analytical framework, not just execution. It moves the relationship from "vendor that runs surveys" to "partner whose thinking we trust."

Evidence:

  • Perxcept is a ZoomRx-specific tool, not a generic research methodology
  • Adoption suggests the Exelixis insights team evaluated ZoomRx's approach and found it credible enough to use for strategic decision-making

Pillar 3: Pipeline Transition Timing

ZoomRx is being engaged at precisely the moment when Exelixis's research needs are expanding most rapidly — the zanzalintinib pre-launch phase. Unbranded campaign impact (market shaping), ATU (market understanding), and branded campaign testing (message optimization) are all pre-launch/launch research priorities. This isn't coincidence; Exelixis is building its research infrastructure for the most important product launch in the company's history, and ZoomRx is in the vendor consideration set.

Evidence:

  • Three distinct zanzalintinib-related engagements in 2026 alone (campaign impact, ATU RFP, branded campaign testing proposal)
  • The shift from cabozantinib work to zanzalintinib work mirrors Exelixis's own strategic pivot

Proven Impact: Case Studies

1. Cabozantinib PET (2019)

Context: ZoomRx's first engagement with Exelixis — a promotional effectiveness tracking study for CABOMETYX in the RCC/HCC market. What it established: ZoomRx's ability to track oncology field force effectiveness in a competitive TKI market. Specific deliverables included physician awareness tracking, message recall measurement, and prescribing intent data. Legacy: This project is the foundation of the relationship. Every subsequent engagement traces back to the credibility built here. The 2023 Integrated Tracking expansion suggests the PET delivered sufficient value to justify deeper investment.

[Populate with specific findings and impact data from project archives.]

2. Exelixis Perxcept (2024)

Context: A specialty research engagement using ZoomRx's proprietary Perxcept methodology. What it established: ZoomRx's ability to deliver differentiated, proprietary insights beyond standard PET/ATU work. Perxcept adoption signals that Exelixis values ZoomRx's methodological innovation. Legacy: Creates a proof point for selling other ZoomRx proprietary methods (Message Labs, AI Dynamic Dialogues) to Exelixis.

[Populate with specific findings and impact data from project archives.]

3. Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact (2026)

Context: Two-wave study measuring the impact of unbranded (disease awareness) campaigns as part of zanzalintinib pre-launch market shaping. What it established: ZoomRx's relevance to the zanzalintinib era — not just a cabozantinib vendor. The multi-wave design (Wave 01 + Wave 02) suggests a tracking commitment, not a one-off study. Legacy: This is the beachhead for all zanzalintinib work. The quality of this deliverable directly influences whether Exelixis awards the ATU and branded campaign testing work to ZoomRx.

[Populate with specific findings and impact data as waves complete.]

Growth Trajectory

The relationship is expanding along a clear vector: from cabozantinib maintenance research to zanzalintinib launch infrastructure.

Phase 1 (2019-2024): Cabozantinib Era

  • PET, Integrated Tracking, Perxcept
  • Revenue: moderate, episodic
  • Relationship: established but not deeply entrenched

Phase 2 (2026-present): Zanzalintinib Pre-Launch

  • Unbranded Campaign Impact (active)
  • ATU (RFP in hand)
  • Branded Campaign Testing (proposal submitted)
  • Revenue: growing — multiple concurrent workstreams
  • Relationship: deepening through active engagement and competitive bids

Phase 3 (Target: 2026-2028): Zanzalintinib Launch and Beyond

  • If ZoomRx wins the ATU and campaign testing: ZoomRx becomes embedded in the zanzalintinib commercial launch infrastructure. This creates a multi-year tracking relationship.
  • Adjacent services to add: PET (launch tracking), MSL/MEE (oncology MSLs are critical for TKI differentiation), Launch KPI, Digital Tracking, HCP-Patient Conversations.
  • Revenue potential: A full zanzalintinib launch tracking suite (PET + ATU + Campaign Testing + MSL + Launch KPI) could represent $400K-$700K+ annually. [UNVERIFIED estimate — based on cross-account benchmarks.]

Competitive Threats to the Relationship

Threat 1: Multi-Vendor RFP Dynamics

The zanzalintinib ATU RFP was issued formally — which typically means Exelixis is evaluating multiple vendors. ZoomRx is not guaranteed the win. Incumbent ATU vendors (Sermo, DRG, others) may be competing.

Mitigation: Differentiate on the ZoomRx value stack — not just ATU in isolation, but ATU that connects to the campaign impact work already running. The integrated insights narrative ("one vendor providing campaign impact + ATU + branded testing = a unified pre-launch intelligence platform") is the competitive moat.

Threat 2: Relationship Discontinuity

There are visible gaps in the timeline (2024-2025). If Exelixis used other vendors during this period, those vendors may have established relationships that ZoomRx needs to displace.

Mitigation: Leverage institutional memory. ZoomRx has 7 years of Exelixis context — knowledge of the cabozantinib franchise, the oncology competitive landscape, and the company's strategic priorities. A new vendor starts from zero; ZoomRx starts from deep context.

Threat 3: Launch Vendor Lock-In

Once Exelixis selects its zanzalintinib launch research partners, those relationships tend to persist through the launch window (2-3 years). If ZoomRx misses the current RFP/proposal cycle, re-entry becomes much harder.

Mitigation: Treat the current ATU and branded campaign testing proposals as must-win opportunities. Deploy senior attention, offer competitive pricing, and emphasize the incumbency advantage from active campaign impact work.

Threat 4: Budget Concentration Risk

Exelixis is a single-franchise company. If zanzalintinib trials fail or are significantly delayed, the entire pre-launch research budget could contract — taking ZoomRx revenue with it.

Mitigation: Maintain awareness of zanzalintinib clinical milestones (STELLAR-301, STELLAR-302 readouts). Diversify by proposing cabozantinib lifecycle management research alongside zanzalintinib launch work.

Key Takeaway for New Principals

Exelixis is a focused, high-conviction account — not a diversified portfolio play like J&J or Pfizer. The entire commercial thesis revolves around one question: Will ZoomRx become a core part of the zanzalintinib launch research infrastructure? The historical relationship (7 years, 6 projects) provides credibility. The current pipeline (active campaign impact work, ATU RFP in hand, branded campaign testing proposal submitted) provides momentum. The strategic imperative is clear: win the ATU, win the branded campaign testing, and use those as the foundation for proposing PET, MSL/MEE, and Launch KPI tracking — building a multi-service, multi-year zanzalintinib partnership that could make this account one of ZoomRx's most valuable oncology relationships.

The risk is equally clear: if ZoomRx loses the current RFP cycle, another vendor gets embedded during launch, and re-entry becomes very difficult. This is a time-sensitive account.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
